sections.paragraph:
διατελέω 1 aor. διετέλεσα Jer 20:7; trans. ‘complete’ (TestJob 41:4 κλαυθμόν [but κλαίων δ. cod. V]; Mel., P. 15, 95 διατελέσας τὸ μυστήριον) intr. continue, remain w. ptc. or adj. to denote the state in which one remains (Hdt.+; Thu. 1, 34, 3 ἀσφαλέστατος διατελεῖ; Diod S 11, 49, 4 καλῶς πολιτευόμενοι διετέλεσαν; ins [freq. of generous public service, s. SIG index] and pap, e.g., PHib 35, 5; UPZ 59, 4 [168 b.c.]; BGU 287, 6–8 ἀεὶ θύων τοῖς θεοῖς διετέλεσα; 2 Macc 5:27; Dt 9:7; EpArist 187; Philo, Aet. M. 19; 93; Jos., Ant. 15, 263, Vi. 423; Ath. 11, 2, R. 79, 2) ἄσιτοι διατελεῖτε you have been continually without food Ac 27:33 (Aristotle in Apollon. Paradox. 25 ἄποτος διετέλεσεν; B-D-F §414, 1).—DELG s.v. τελέω. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

trans. trans. = transitive

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

intr. intr. = intransitive

ptc. ptc. = participle

adj. adj. = adjective

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 238
